result.summary: Found 4 issues on the BetRivers Pennsylvania page, ranging from minor to major. The most notable problems involve truncated UI elements and partially clipped game cards that may confuse users.

issues.description: The horizontal navigation tab 'Hot Ga...' is cut off at the right edge of the screen, obscuring the full label (likely 'Hot Games'). This makes the tab label unreadable.
issues.suggestion: Ensure navigation tabs are either fully visible or implement a proper overflow scroll indicator with ellipsis that doesn't cut through the middle of words. Consider reducing tab padding or font size.

issues.description: Multiple game card titles in the 'BetRivers Exclusive Games' section are abruptly clipped on the right side (e.g., 'MAGNI PHO...' and 'MUNC MI...'), making them unreadable and appearing broken rather than intentionally scrollable.
issues.suggestion: Add a visible scroll affordance (e.g., arrow button, gradient fade, or scrollbar) to clearly indicate horizontal scrollability. The cards should either fully show or clearly hint at more content.

issues.description: In the 'Top Played New Games' section, game cards on the right side are also clipped (e.g., 'HYPERA... PYRAMID...' and 'DAR WATE THE CURS...'), making titles unreadable.
issues.suggestion: Same as above — add scroll affordance and ensure partial cards are visually designed to imply scrollability rather than appearing as rendering errors.

issues.description: The truncated navigation and game card labels provide no alternative text or tooltip for screen readers or users who cannot infer the full content from partial text.
issues.suggestion: Add aria-labels or title attributes to truncated elements so screen readers can announce the full name, improving accessibility compliance.
